summary: preinstall script fetches content from non-publisher host and references system/user info
details: The package's dist/scripts/preinstall.js is executed at install time (npm lifecycle hook) and performs outbound HTTPS/HTTP requests to `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name`, a host that does not match the package publisher (`@gusmano/reext`). The same script requires `os`, `fs`, `https`, and `http`, calls `os.userInfo()` and `os.networkInterfaces()`, and reads `process.env`, then issues `https.get(...)` to the external host. This matches the known install-time-rce / exfiltration shape: a preinstall script that collects host/user/network metadata and contacts a non-publisher, non-registry endpoint at install time. The destination domain (`l.serverhost.name` subdomain under a dynamic-DNS-style host) is not a documented publisher CDN, package registry, or known runtime source, and the fetch is not version-pinned or hash-verified. No legitimate build, native-addon, or vendor-SDK shape is apparent from the evidence. Given the preinstall trigger (runs on `npm install` before any code review), the collection of host identity data, and the contact with an undocumented external host, this is an installer-harm pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
